hook: You already know a lot of powerful math — number theory, combinatorics, geometry, and more. But in a timed competition, knowing the math is only half the battle; the other half is knowing *how* to think when you're stuck, when the clock is ticking, and when the answer isn't obvious. What separates competitors who score well from those who don't is often not raw knowledge, but a toolkit of flexible problem-solving strategies.
concept: This unit builds the *meta-skills* of mathematical competition: the habits of mind and tactical decisions that let you unlock problems efficiently, recover from dead ends, and perform under pressure — collectively known as 'competition strategy.'

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to crack seemingly intractable problems by starting from the desired answer or testing simple special cases to reveal hidden structure.
Master the art of spotting symmetry, invariants, and repeating patterns that dramatically simplify calculations and eliminate unnecessary casework.
Apply upper and lower bound techniques to narrow down answer choices, verify solutions, and make confident progress even when an exact computation is out of reach.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Time Management and Partial Credit Strategy
topic_roadmap.description: Develop a tactical approach to allocating your time across problems and earning maximum points by recognizing when to move on and how to present partial solutions effectively.
Bring everything together by competing under realistic timed conditions, diagnosing your strengths and weaknesses, and refining your strategy through full-length practice contests.
